skill.name: wan-2-7
skill.description: Generate text-to-video with Wan 2.7 (Wan-AI's flagship motion model) on RunComfy. Documents Wan 2.7's strengths (multi-reference conditioning, audio-driven lip-sync via `audio_url`, smoother transitions, prompt expansion), the duration / resolution / aspect-ratio schema, and when to route to HappyHorse 1.0 / Seedance 2.0 / Kling / LTX 2 instead. Calls `runcomfy run wan-ai/wan-2-7/text-to-video` through the local RunComfy CLI. Triggers on "wan", "wan 2.7", "wan-2-7", "wan video", or any explicit ask to generate video with this model.
skill.summary: Generate high-quality text-to-video content with Wan 2.7 AI model featuring lip-sync, multi-reference control, and smooth motion on RunComfy
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 72 potential issues, primarily Ruby backtick patterns in markdown documentation. Manual review confirms all external_command detections are false positives - the backticks are markdown code formatting, not actual code execution. The skill is a legitimate RunComfy CLI wrapper for video generation. No command injection vulnerabilities exist. Network calls are to documented RunComfy API endpoints only. Token storage uses secure file permissions (0600).

medium_findings.title: External Command Execution Documentation
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md documents CLI commands using backtick syntax for markdown formatting. The static scanner flagged these as 'Ruby backtick execution' patterns. All 58 detections are false positives - the skill contains only documentation examples, not executable code. The RunComfy CLI is invoked with structured JSON input, not shell-expanded prompts.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All flagged patterns are markdown documentation examples with backtick-wrapped CLI commands, not Ruby code. The skill file contains no Ruby interpreter or shell execution logic.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Service URLs
low_findings.description: Documentation references hardcoded RunComfy service URLs (runcomfy.com, model-api.runcomfy.net). These are expected for a platform-specific CLI wrapper and pose no security risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs are documented service endpoints for the RunComfy platform, not malicious or exfiltration targets.

low_findings.title: Local Token Storage Path
low_findings.description: Token stored at ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with mode 0600 permissions. This is secure local credential storage with proper file permissions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Token storage uses owner-only read/write permissions (0600), which is the secure approach for credential storage.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer Crypto/Reconnaissance Flags
low_findings.description: The static scanner flagged weak crypto algorithms at lines 4, 112, 162 and system reconnaissance at line 39. Manual review shows these are documentation text - displayName, negative prompt guidance, and trigger keywords respectively. No cryptographic operations or reconnaissance activities exist.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Scanner misidentified documentation text as code patterns. No actual crypto or system reconnaissance in this skill.

content.user_title: Generate AI Videos with Wan 2.7 on RunComfy
content.value_statement: Create professional text-to-video content with Wan 2.7 AI. Generate lip-synced videos, cinematic motion, and smooth transitions using simple text prompts through the RunComfy CLI integration.
actual_capabilities: Generate 2-15 second videos from text prompts up to 5000 characters
Create lip-synced videos by providing audio tracks (WAV/MP3, 3-30 seconds)
Apply negative prompts to exclude unwanted elements from generated videos
Enable automatic prompt expansion for enhanced video quality
Generate consistent variations by reusing seeds across generations
limitations: Maximum video duration is 15 seconds per generation
Audio files must be 3-30 seconds, WAV/MP3 format, under 15MB

use_cases.title: Create Lip-Synced Marketing Videos
use_cases.description: Upload a voiceover track to generate a spokesperson video that perfectly matches your audio. Ideal for localized ads and multi-language campaigns.

use_cases.title: Generate Cinematic Product Showcases
use_cases.description: Create smooth, professional product videos with camera motion and lighting control using simple text descriptions.

use_cases.title: Produce Platform-Native Short-Form Content
use_cases.description: Generate vertical videos optimized for TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts with proper aspect ratios.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Product Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a video of [product name] on a [surface type], [lighting description], [camera motion]
prompt_templates.scenario: When users want simple product showcase videos

prompt_templates.title: Lip-Synced Spokesperson
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a video with a [shot type] of [description], [lighting setup], [camera movement]. Include audio_sync with [audio_url]
prompt_templates.scenario: When users provide a voiceover track for lip-sync

prompt_templates.title: Vertical Social Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 9:16 vertical video showing [action/scene description], [mood/tone], [camera style]
prompt_templates.scenario: When users need short-form vertical content

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Reference Motion Control
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate video using [number] reference [media types], focusing on [motion type], with [style description]
prompt_templates.scenario: When users want precise motion control with multiple reference inputs

output_examples.input: Cinematic medium shot of a coffee mug on a marble surface, soft studio lighting, slow subtle camera push-in, shallow depth of field
output_examples.output: A 5-second video showing a coffee mug with smooth camera movement, professional lighting, and clean background suitable for product photography

output_examples.input: 9:16 vertical short. A barista pulls a single espresso shot, steam rising into morning sun, rich crema slowly forming. Close-up handheld
output_examples.output: A vertical video optimized for social media showing the espresso preparation with warm lighting and cinematic close-up handheld camera work

output_examples.input: Medium close-up spokesperson, warm key light, locked tripod, slight breathing motion. Audio: professional voiceover.mp3
output_examples.output: A lip-synced video where the speaker's mouth movements match the provided audio track with professional studio lighting

best_practices: Front-load your prompt with camera and motion instructions for better control over the generated video
Use negative prompts to exclude specific issues like watermarks, subtitles, or visual artifacts
Iterate with different seeds when you want variations of the same concept
anti_patterns: Avoid static frame descriptions without motion - the model needs movement cues to generate meaningful video
Do not use vague negative prompts like 'no bad colors' - be specific about issues to avoid
Never use audio outside the 3-30 second or 15MB limits - out-of-spec files will be rejected

faq.question: What is the maximum video length I can generate?
faq.answer: Wan 2.7 supports 2-15 second videos. For longer content, you can stitch multiple generations together.

faq.question: Can I use my own audio track for lip-sync?
faq.answer: Yes, provide an audio_url with your request. The file must be WAV or MP3, 3-30 seconds long, and under 15MB.

faq.question: How do I get consistent results across multiple generations?
faq.answer: Use the same seed value to reproduce identical conditions. Change the seed only when you want genuine variety.

faq.question: What resolution options are available?
faq.answer: Wan 2.7 supports 720p and 1080p resolution. The default is 1080p.

faq.question: Can I control when prompt expansion happens?
faq.answer: Yes, set enable_prompt_expansion to false if you need literal control over your prompt and do not want auto-rewriting.

faq.question: What happens if my audio file exceeds the size limit?
faq.answer: Files over 15MB or outside the 3-30 second range will be rejected by the API. Ensure your audio meets these specifications.
